title: Assessment of Clinical Criteria for Sepsis For the Third International Consensus Definitions for Sepsis and Septic Shock (Sepsis-3)
country: United States (primary cohort); also Germany and out-of-hospital US settings in external cohorts

sample_size.source_quote: In the primary cohort, 148 907 encounters had suspected infection (n = 74 453 derivation; n = 74 454 validation), of whom 6347 (4%) died.

sofa_score_baseline.source_quote:
SOFA near onset of suspected infectiond
Mean (SD)
2.0 (2.7)

icu_mortality_percent.source_quote: Among ICU encounters in the validation cohort (n = 7932 with suspected infection, of whom 1289 [16%] died)

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.model_context: not specified
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Among ICU encounters in the validation cohort (n = 7932 with suspected infection, of whom 1289 [16%] died), the predictive validity for in-hospital mortality was lower for SIRS (AUROC = 0.64; 95% CI, 0.62-0.66) and qSOFA (AUROC = 0.66; 95% CI, 0.64-0.68) vs SOFA (AUROC = 0.74; 95% CI, 0.73-0.76; P < .001 for both) or LODS (AUROC = 0.75; 95% CI, 0.73-0.76; P < .001 for both).

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.model_context: not specified
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Among ICU encounters in the validation cohort (n = 7932 with suspected infection, of whom 1289 [16%] died), the predictive validity for in-hospital mortality was lower for SIRS (AUROC = 0.64; 95% CI, 0.62-0.66) and qSOFA (AUROC = 0.66; 95% CI, 0.64-0.68) vs SOFA (AUROC = 0.74; 95% CI, 0.73-0.76; P < .001 for both) or LODS (AUROC = 0.75; 95% CI, 0.73-0.76; P < .001 for both).

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.model_context: not specified
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Among ICU encounters in the validation cohort (n = 7932 with suspected infection, of whom 1289 [16%] died), the predictive validity for in-hospital mortality was lower for SIRS (AUROC = 0.64; 95% CI, 0.62-0.66) and qSOFA (AUROC = 0.66; 95% CI, 0.64-0.68) vs SOFA (AUROC = 0.74; 95% CI, 0.73-0.76; P < .001 for both) or LODS (AUROC = 0.75; 95% CI, 0.73-0.76; P < .001 for both).

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.model_context: not specified; non-ICU encounters
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Among non-ICU encounters in the validation cohort (n = 66 522 with suspected infection, of whom 1886 [3%] died), qSOFA had predictive validity (AUROC = 0.81; 95% CI, 0.80-0.82) that was greater than SOFA (AUROC = 0.79; 95% CI, 0.78-0.80; P < .001) and SIRS (AUROC = 0.76; 95% CI, 0.75-0.77; P < .001).

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.model_context: not specified; non-ICU encounters
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Among non-ICU encounters in the validation cohort (n = 66 522 with suspected infection, of whom 1886 [3%] died), qSOFA had predictive validity (AUROC = 0.81; 95% CI, 0.80-0.82) that was greater than SOFA (AUROC = 0.79; 95% CI, 0.78-0.80; P < .001) and SIRS (AUROC = 0.76; 95% CI, 0.75-0.77; P < .001).

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.statistical_method: fold change in outcome over deciles of baseline risk
mortality_predictors.model_context: not specified; non-ICU encounters
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Relative to qSOFA scores lower than 2, encounters with qSOFA scores of 2 or higher had a 3- to 14-fold increase in hospital mortality across baseline risk deciles.

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.model_context: not specified; ICU encounters, UPMC validation cohort
mortality_predictors.source_quote: In the ICU, the predictive validity for hospital mortality using SOFA (AUROC = 0.74; 95% CI, 0.73-0.76) and LODS (AUROC = 0.75; 95% CI, 0.73-0.76; P = .20) were not statistically different but were statistically greater than that of SIRS (AUROC = 0.64; 95% CI, 0.62-0.66; P < .001 for either LODS or SOFA vs SIRS)

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.model_context: not specified; ICU encounters, UPMC validation cohort
mortality_predictors.source_quote: In the ICU, the predictive validity for hospital mortality using SOFA (AUROC = 0.74; 95% CI, 0.73-0.76) and LODS (AUROC = 0.75; 95% CI, 0.73-0.76; P = .20) were not statistically different but were statistically greater than that of SIRS (AUROC = 0.64; 95% CI, 0.62-0.66; P < .001 for either LODS or SOFA vs SIRS)

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.model_context: not specified; ICU encounters
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The SOFA score was 2 or more in 98% of decedents (95% CI, 97%-99%); among survivors, the SOFA score was less than 2 in 10% (95% CI, 10%-11%).

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.model_context: not specified; non-ICU encounters
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Seventy percent (95% CI, 69%-72%) of decedents had 2 or more qSOFA points and 78% (95% CI, 78%-79%) of survivors had less than 2 qSOFA points (eTable 3 in the Supplement).

mortality_predictors.timing: near onset of infection
mortality_predictors.statistical_method: AUROC comparison with and without lactate added to qSOFA
mortality_predictors.model_context: qSOFA + lactate model; KPNC data
mortality_predictors.source_quote: the post hoc addition of serum lactate levels of 2.0 mmol/L (18 mg/dL) or more to qSOFA (revised to a 4-point score with 1 added point for elevated serum lactate level) statistically changed the predictive validity of qSOFA (AUROC with lactate = 0.80; 95% CI, 0.79-0.81 vs AUROC without lactate = 0.79; 95% CI, 0.78-0.80; P < .001)

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.model_context: not specified; non-ICU encounters, UPMC validation cohort
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The discrimination of hospital mortality using SOFA (AUROC = 0.79;95%CI,0.78-0.80),LODS(AUROC = 0.82;95% CI, 0.81-0.83), or change in SOFA (AUROC = 0.79; 95% CI, 0.78-0.79) scores was significantly greater compared with SIRS criteria (AUROC = 0.76; 95% CI, 0.75-0.77; P < .01 for all)

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.model_context: not specified; non-ICU encounters, UPMC validation cohort
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The discrimination of hospital mortality using SOFA (AUROC = 0.79;95%CI,0.78-0.80),LODS(AUROC = 0.82;95% CI, 0.81-0.83), or change in SOFA (AUROC = 0.79; 95% CI, 0.78-0.79) scores was significantly greater compared with SIRS criteria (AUROC = 0.76; 95% CI, 0.75-0.77; P < .01 for all)

mortality_predictors.timing: 48 hours before to 24 hours after onset of infection
mortality_predictors.model_context: not specified; non-ICU encounters, UPMC validation cohort; compared to SOFA and SIRS
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The predictive validity of qSOFA was good for in-hospital mortality (AUROC = 0.81; 95% CI, 0.80-0.82), was not statistically different from LODS (P = .77) and was statistically greater than SOFA or change in SOFA score (P < .001 for both)

mortality_predictors.timing: ICU encounters
mortality_predictors.model_context: not specified; ICU encounters, UPMC validation cohort
mortality_predictors.source_quote: In the ICU, the predictive validity for hospital mortality of qSOFA above baseline risk (AUROC = 0.66; 95% CI, 0.64-0.68) was statistically greater than SIRS criteria (P = .01) but significantly less than SOFA (P < .001)
